(born 1987 or 1988)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is an English actor, best known for his roles as [Chris Miles](/wiki/Chris_Miles_(Skins) "Chris Miles (Skins)") in the [E4](/wiki/E4_(TV_channel) "E4 (TV channel)") teen comedy-drama *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* (2007–2008) and [Gendry](/wiki/Gendry "Gendry") in *[Game of Thrones](/wiki/Game_of_Thrones "Game of Thrones")* (2011–2013; 2017–2019).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Joseph Dempsie](/w/index.php?title=Joseph_Dempsie&redirect=no "Joseph Dempsie"))

English actor

| Joe Dempsie | |
| --- | --- |
| Dempsie in 2018 | |
| Born | 1987 or 1988 (age 37–38) |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Years active | 2000–present |

**Joe Dempsie** (born 1987 or 1988)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is an English actor, best known for his roles as [Chris Miles](/wiki/Chris_Miles_(Skins) "Chris Miles (Skins)") in the [E4](/wiki/E4_(TV_channel) "E4 (TV channel)") teen comedy-drama *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* (2007–2008) and [Gendry](/wiki/Gendry "Gendry") in *[Game of Thrones](/wiki/Game_of_Thrones "Game of Thrones")* (2011–2013; 2017–2019).

Dempsie's earlier acting credits include the medical dramas *[Peak Practice](/wiki/Peak_Practice "Peak Practice")* (2000), *[Doctors](/wiki/Doctors_(2000_TV_series) "Doctors (2000 TV series)")* (2001–2003), and *[Sweet Medicine](/wiki/Sweet_Medicine "Sweet Medicine")* (2003), as well as the films *One for the Road* and *[Heartlands](/wiki/Heartlands_(film) "Heartlands (film)")* (2003). He also appeared in *[This is England '86](/wiki/This_is_England_%2786 "This is England '86")* (2010) and *[This is England '90](/wiki/This_is_England_%2790 "This is England '90")* (2015), *[Born and Bred](/wiki/Born_and_Bred "Born and Bred")*, a [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC") documentary-drama about [Tony Martin](/wiki/Tony_Martin_(farmer) "Tony Martin (farmer)"), and as the villainous John in *[The Fades](/wiki/The_Fades_(TV_series) "The Fades (TV series)")* (2011).

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Joe_Dempsie&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Dempsie's father was a [social worker](/wiki/Social_worker "Social worker") and his mother an [educational psychologist](/wiki/Educational_psychologist "Educational psychologist").[[2]](#cite_note-gq-2) He grew up in [West Bridgford, Nottinghamshire](/wiki/West_Bridgford "West Bridgford").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was educated at [West Bridgford School](/wiki/West_Bridgford_School "West Bridgford School"), and received acting training from the [Central Junior Television Workshop](/wiki/Central_Junior_Television_Workshop "Central Junior Television Workshop") in [Nottingham](/wiki/Nottingham "Nottingham").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Joe_Dempsie&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Dempsie has been the voice of the [Clearasil](/wiki/Clearasil "Clearasil") advertisements in the UK.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-Stupid-6) In episode 6 of the fourth series of *[Doctor Who](/wiki/Doctor_Who "Doctor Who")*, "[The Doctor's Daughter](/wiki/The_Doctor%27s_Daughter "The Doctor's Daughter")", that aired on 10 May 2008, Dempsie played the character Cline,[[7]](#cite_note-Radio_Times-7) a soldier who watches his comrades die.[[8]](#cite_note-8) On 18 April 2008, Dempsie appeared on *[Friday Night Project](/wiki/Friday_Night_Project "Friday Night Project")* with [Geri Halliwell](/wiki/Geri_Halliwell "Geri Halliwell").[[9]](#cite_note-9) On 20 July 2008, he appeared at [T4 on the Beach](/wiki/T4_on_the_Beach "T4 on the Beach") in [Weston Super Mare](/wiki/Weston_Super_Mare "Weston Super Mare") alongside fellow *Skins* cast members. He appeared as [Duncan McKenzie](/wiki/Duncan_McKenzie "Duncan McKenzie") in 2009's *[The Damned United](/wiki/The_Damned_United "The Damned United")* alongside [Michael Sheen](/wiki/Michael_Sheen "Michael Sheen"), [Jim Broadbent](/wiki/Jim_Broadbent "Jim Broadbent"), [Stephen Graham](/wiki/Stephen_Graham_(actor) "Stephen Graham (actor)") and [Timothy Spall](/wiki/Timothy_Spall "Timothy Spall"). In November 2008, he appeared in ["The Moment of Truth"](/wiki/The_Moment_of_Truth_(Merlin) "The Moment of Truth (Merlin)"), episode 10 of the BBC show *[Merlin](/wiki/Merlin_(TV_series) "Merlin (TV series)")*. He played Will, who is an old friend of Merlin's, from the village in which they grew up together.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f8/Joseph_Dempsie_2007.jpg/250px-Joseph_Dempsie_2007.jpg)](/wiki/File:Joseph_Dempsie_2007.jpg)

Joe Dempsie at Skins Party 2007

In 2010, he voiced Steven, a gay teenager from Nottinghamshire in the Radio 4 play *Once Upon a Time*.[[6]](#cite_note-Stupid-6) He plays the character of [Gendry Baratheon](/wiki/Gendry "Gendry") in HBO's series *[Game of Thrones](/wiki/Game_of_Thrones "Game of Thrones")* based on [George R. R. Martin](/wiki/George_R._R._Martin "George R. R. Martin")'s *[A Song of Ice and Fire](/wiki/A_Song_of_Ice_and_Fire "A Song of Ice and Fire")* novels.[[10]](#cite_note-10) He appeared in the first three, seventh and eighth of its eight seasons. He also appeared in the [Channel 4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4") mini-series *[This Is England '86](/wiki/This_Is_England_%2786 "This Is England '86")*, as well as the follow-ups *[This Is England '88](/wiki/This_Is_England_%2788 "This Is England '88")* and *[This Is England '90](/wiki/This_Is_England_%2790 "This Is England '90")*. He has also appeared in *[Harry & Paul](/wiki/Harry_%26_Paul "Harry & Paul")* on BBC Two, appearing as a northerner who was presented as a gift from the character Harry was playing to his daughter.[[11]](#cite_note-11) Recently, he has appeared in the [BBC Three](/wiki/BBC_Three "BBC Three") series *[The Fades](/wiki/The_Fades_(TV_series) "The Fades (TV series)")* (episodes 3 to 6), as John.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In January 2021, it was announced that Dempsie was cast as Nick in the upcoming [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") thriller series *[Pieces of Her](/wiki/Pieces_of_Her_(TV_series) "Pieces of Her (TV series)")*, which is adapted from the [Karin Slaughter](/wiki/Karin_Slaughter "Karin Slaughter") [novel of the same name](/wiki/Pieces_of_Her "Pieces of Her").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Joe_Dempsie&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

As of 2015, Dempsie was residing in [London](/wiki/London "London").[[14]](#cite_note-14)

He is a [Nottingham Forest F.C.](/wiki/Nottingham_Forest_F.C. "Nottingham Forest F.C.") fan.[[15]](#cite_note-15)
